stem: An architect's scale drawing uses a ratio of 1 inch to 3 feet. On the drawing, a rectangular room measures 5 inches by 4 inches. What is the actual area of the room in square feet?
explanation:
Step 1: Convert each dimension from inches to feet using the scale (1 inch = 3 feet).
$$\text{Actual length} = 5 \times 3 = 15 \text{ feet}$$
$$\text{Actual width} = 4 \times 3 = 12 \text{ feet}$$

Step 2: Calculate the actual area.
$$\text{Area} = 15 \times 12 = 180 \text{ square feet}$$

distractorRationale.A: Stopped at intermediate value — the student correctly scales one dimension ($5 \times 3 = 15$ feet) but reports that single dimension as the final answer rather than computing the area of both dimensions.
distractorRationale.B: Scaling error — the student first computes the area of the drawing in square inches ($5 \times 4 = 20$ sq in) and then applies the scale factor only once (multiplying by 3) instead of applying it to each dimension separately, which would be equivalent to multiplying by $3^2 = 9$. Wrong calculation: $20 \times 3 = 60$ square feet.
distractorRationale.C: Correct answer. Actual length $= 5 \times 3 = 15$ ft; actual width $= 4 \times 3 = 12$ ft; area $= 15 \times 12 = 180$ sq ft.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong unit conversion factor — the student misremembers the scale as 1 inch = 4 feet instead of 1 inch = 3 feet. Wrong calculation: actual length $= 5 \times 4 = 20$ ft; actual width $= 4 \times 4 = 16$ ft; area $= 20 \times 16 = 320$ square feet.
